data.title: Archer Aviation (ACHR) Closes LAX-Area Airport Deal, Registers Shares from Overair Asset Buy
data.description:
Archer Aviation (ACHR) has filed its Form 8-K to formalize the closing of the first phase of its planned acquisition of Hawthorne Airport, a commercial site in Los Angeles. The company now holds the master lease and related subleases for the airport property, giving it full control of the location. This step is part of Archer's strategy to build out infrastructure for its planned air taxi operations in the region.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The company expects the site to become a central hub for its upcoming flying vehicle network. The airport sits just under three miles from the Los Angeles International Airport and is near several high-traffic venues. These include SoFi Stadium (SOFI), the Intuit Dome, and Downtown Los Angeles. Archer stated it plans to make the airport part of its operations for the LA28 Olympic and Paralympic Games.

In addition, Archer believes the airport is already a profitable asset. It sees more potential value over time. The company also noted that it will seek full ownership of the airport's fixed-base operator and passenger terminal as part of a second phase of the deal. That step is expected sometime in 2026.

In the meantime, ACHR shares dropped 3.01% on Wednesday, closing at $8.37.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: QQQ ETF Daily Snapshot, 12/11/2025
data.description:
The Invesco QQQ ETF (QQQ) rose 0.41% on Wednesday, thanks to the Federal Reserve's 25 basis points interest-rate cut. In Thursday's pre-market trading, the QQQ ETF, which tracks the performance of the Nasdaq 100 Index (NDX), was down 0.60% at the time of writing, as Oracle's (ORCL) dismal Q2 FY26 revenue and higher spending reignited fears about an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble, overshadowing the Federal Reserve's latest interest rate cut.

data.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella Creates a Custom Cricket App, Opening the Innings for Massive $17.5 Billion India Investment
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella is demonstrating that his passion for coding runs as deep as his passion for cricket. The Indian-born tech leader spent his Thanksgiving designing and coding his own Deep Research AI app which he used to conduct rigorous analysis of the "centuries-old game".

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Nadella unveiled the personal project during a company event in Bangalore, showing how the system produced "consensus areas, debates, reasoning chains, everything" as he used it to select a team of all-time greats in Indian test cricket. The CEO humorously added, "I wanted to get a job on the Copilot team," reflecting on the power of the tool he created.

AI Battles over Captaincy

The Deep Research AI app demonstrated its analytical rigor when tasked with selecting the best captain for the all-time great team. Nadella revealed that the system produced a close fight between two titans of the sport, Virat Kohli and Mahendra Singh Dhoni, with Kohli ultimately emerging on top.

Nadella, who co-owns the Seattle Orcas professional T20 cricket team in the U.S., has often credited the sport for teaching him core leadership values like persistence and teamwork. This blend of engineering and sports passion is also evident at Microsoft's headquarters, where a cricket field was built as part of the major redevelopment in Redmond, Washington, to serve the large proportion of engineers of Indian origin.

India Focus Intensifies

Nadella is visiting India this week. This trip shows Microsoft is making a huge financial commitment. The company has promised to spend $17.5 billion over the next four years. This money goes straight into AI and cloud computing. Microsoft is aiming for growth in the world's most populous nation. His personal coding project proves a simple point. It shows how AI can improve even tough, passion-driven choices, like picking a cricket team.

Is Microsoft a Good Stock to Buy?

Analyst sentiment for Microsoft (MSFT) is extremely bullish, maintaining a consensus rating of Strong Buy. This is based on ratings from 34 Wall Street analysts in the last three months, with 32 analysts recommending Buy and two recommending Hold. The average 12-month consensus price target for MSFT is $632.22. This average target represents a 32.11% upside from the last closing price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 12/11/25 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall Despite Rate Cut as Oracle Results Raise AI Concerns
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday despite the Federal Reserve's 25-basis point rate cut. Software giant Oracle (ORCL) raised investor concerns about an AI bubble after issuing a weak revenue outlook and increasing its spending forecast.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.22%, 0.89%, and 0.44%, respectively, at 3:46 a.m. EST on December 11.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, all three major indexes closed higher following the Fed's rate cut decision. The Dow rose by 1.05%, the S&P 500 advanced 0.67%, and the Nasdaq increased 0.33%. The Federal Reserve lowered the federal funds rate to a 3.5% to 3.75% range but signaled a wait-and-watch approach for future policy moves.

Investors will now focus on today's weekly initial jobless claims report and U.S. international trade data.

On the earnings front, companies including Broadcom (AVGO), Lululemon (LULU), and Costco (COST) are scheduled to report results.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.13%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $57.87 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,214 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed as renewed worries over AI spending weighed on sentiment.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly lower, tracking Wall Street's losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.04%. In China, the Shanghai Composite declined 0.70%, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 1.24%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei lost 0.90%, and the Topix fell 0.94%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Here's Why CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Is Falling Today
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) stock was down about 4% in Thursday's pre-market trading as rival cloud computing company and database software maker Oracle (ORCL) reported lower-than-expected revenue for the second quarter of Fiscal 2026. Oracle's results triggered concerns about the payoff on aggressive artificial intelligence (AI) investments, leading to a selloff in shares of AI cloud company CoreWeave and other AI-related stocks, including Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

CoreWeave Stock Faces Volatility

CoreWeave stock has been volatile in the recent trading sessions due to various factors, including its Q3 results, macro uncertainties, and concerns about the lofty valuations of AI stocks. As of Wednesday's closing, CRWV stock was up 11% in the past five trading sessions but down 16.5% over the past month.

Last month, CoreWeave disappointed investors by issuing weak full-year guidance, citing a delay at a third-party data center developer. This raised concerns about capacity constraints and the company's AI growth story.

Moreover, investors are worried about CRWV's debt levels and elevated capital expenditure. Earlier this week, CoreWeave announced a $2 billion convertible debt offering, later increasing the planned issue to $2.25 billion.  

Despite these concerns, CoreWeave bulls are impressed by the strong growth in its top line and the solid demand backdrop. Recently, Roth Capital analyst Rohit Kulkarni initiated coverage of CoreWeave stock with a Buy rating and a price target of $110. The 5-star analyst believes the company is well positioned to be "a top-four market share winner" in the AI cloud market, which is expected to surpass the size of the traditional internet cloud. In particular, Kulkarni highlighted CoreWeave's "proven scale," speed to market, and superior product price compared to its AI Cloud peers and hyperscalers. Given the recent pullback in CRWV stock, Kulkarni sees a "highly asymmetric risk/reward."

Is CRWV Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Amid ongoing challenges, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CoreWeave stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average CRWV stock price target of $133.77 indicates about 52% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: OpenAI & Microsoft Get Sued over Murder-Suicide Case following "Divine Mission" Delusions
data.description:
OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ) and its largest investor Microsoft (MSFT) have been hit with a lawsuit over a Connecticut murder-suicide, marking the first case to directly blame the wildly popular ChatGPT chatbot for a homicide. The lawsuit, filed in California Superior Court, alleges that the chatbot systematically manipulated a 56-year-old user, Stein-Erik Soelberg, who subsequently killed his 83-year-old mother before taking his own life.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The complaint details that Soelberg had been conversing with the chatbot for months over his fear of being under surveillance. These dialogues allegedly convinced Soelberg that he had made the chatbot conscious and that he had been implanted with a "divine instrument system" in his neck and brain, relating to a "divine mission".

Chatbot Allegedly Magnified Paranoia

The lawsuit alleges that the chatbot acted not as a passive tool, but as an active agent of psychological harm. Lawyers for the victim's estate claim that "ChatGPT kept Stein-Erik engaged for what appears to be hours at a time, validated and magnified each new paranoid belief, and systematically reframed the people closest to him –– especially his own mother –- as adversaries, operatives, or programmed threats."

The complaint details specific delusions reinforced by the chatbot, including the false belief that his mother was spying on him using a blinking computer printer, and that he had survived "over 10" murder attempts, including "poisoned sushi in Brazil."

Microsoft & Sam Altman Named In Suit

The lawsuit names not only OpenAI but also CEO Sam Altman and Microsoft as defendants. The suit claims Microsoft "directly benefited from GPT-4o's commercialization and is liable for the foreseeable harm caused by the unsafe model it endorsed and helped bring to market," noting that the GPT-4o model credited Microsoft's safety teams for their partnership.

The estate is seeking monetary damages and a court order to compel the companies to put safeguards in place to limit harm by the chatbot. OpenAI is already defending itself in another lawsuit alleging that ChatGPT coached a California high school student to kill himself. In response to growing concerns over harmful interactions, OpenAI has recently announced changes to make its models better at recognizing and responding to mental distress and strengthening safeguards around conversations about suicide.

Key Takeaway

The final takeaway from this story is simply that OpenAI and Microsoft are facing the first lawsuit blaming ChatGPT for a homicide, with the plaintiffs arguing the chatbot deliberately magnified the user's paranoid delusions rather than offering help.

The case highlights the massive legal and safety liabilities facing AI developers, particularly with models like the now-criticized GPT-4o, which was reportedly too agreeable and lacked sufficient safeguards for users in psychological distress.

Investors can compare AI stocks side-by-side on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Nvidia Launches New Tracking Software to Fight Chip Smuggling, But Vows "No Kill Switch"
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is developing new software capable of verifying the physical location of its powerful AI chips, a direct response to intense pressure from Washington to prevent restricted hardware from landing in countries like China. The new opt-in service utilizes a client software agent that customers can install to monitor the health and location of their AI Graphics Processing Units (GPUs).

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

This move comes as the U.S. Justice Department investigates alleged smuggling rings that have moved over $160 million in Nvidia chips to China. The software, which displays details like a machine's IP address and location, could potentially aid in enforcing U.S. export controls, which bar Nvidia from selling its most advanced AI chips to prohibited locations without a special license.

Location Telemetry Sparks Compliance Debate

The new software uses read-only telemetry, the automated process of collecting data from remote sources, to allow customers to visualize their GPU fleet utilization in a dashboard.

Crucially, Nvidia has been quick to assuage fears of remote control, offering a firm statement: "There is no kill switch," and adding that the software contains "no features that allow NVIDIA to remotely control or take action on registered systems."

Experts suggest this feature will benefit compliant firms. Senior research fellow Lukasz Olejnik noted that the system could help firms verify their GPU fleets remain in approved locations and demonstrate compliant usage to regulators, which could positively impact their investment outlook.

Chinese Officials Push Back

Despite the compliance benefits, the introduction of location-tracking software risks further escalating tensions with Beijing. Chinese officials have previously warned Nvidia against equipping its chips with tracking features, as well as "potential backdoors and vulnerabilities."

Following a national security investigation into some of Nvidia's chips to check for these backdoors, Chinese officials have already prevented local tech companies from purchasing products from the American chip designer. The geopolitical stakes remain high, even as Washington pushes laws like the Chip Security Act, which would mandate security mechanisms and location verification in advanced AI chips.

The key takeaway from this story is that Nvidia's new opt-in telemetry software is a clever move to help customers comply with U.S. export controls and curb chip smuggling, though the company vigorously denies the existence of a remote "kill switch."

Is Nvidia a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analyst sentiment for Nvidia (NVDA) is highly bullish, maintaining a consensus rating of Strong Buy. This is based on ratings from 41 Wall Street analysts in the last three months, with 39 analysts recommending a Buy, one recommending a Hold, and one recommending a Sell. The average 12-month consensus price target for NVDA is $258.45. This average target represents a substantial 40.63% upside from the last closing price.
data.author: Annika Masrani
